engineIntelligence.h2: MG Montego Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the MG Montego in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: MG Montego S-Series 2.0L engine - petrol 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Head gasket failure - coolant/oil mixing common after 80k miles on high-mileage units.
Timing chain tensioner wear - causes rattle and potential oil pressure warnings.
Thermostat housing crack - plastic housing prone to splitting, leading to overheating.

cards.altText: MG Montego Perkins Prima 4.100 engine - diesel 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Injector pump wear - hard starting and power loss common after 100k miles.
Glow plug relay failure - cold start difficulties in winter conditions.
EGR system clogging - causes rough idle and increased emissions on high-mileage units.

cards.altText: MG Montego A+ Series 1.3L engine - petrol 1.3L replacement UK
knownFailures: Head gasket MLS upgrade recommended - original gasket prone to failure on high-mileage units.
Carburettor flooding - SU carb choke mechanism can stick, causing rich running.
Oil seal wear - rocker cover and sump gaskets commonly leak after 70k miles.

engineGuide.h2: MG Montego Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the MG Montego across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.3L Petrol
entries.history: The A+ Series powered entry-level Montegos, offering simple carburettor reliability and easy maintenance for budget-conscious classic car owners.
commonFailures: Head gasket failure - original gasket prone to coolant/oil mixing after 70k miles
SU carburettor flooding - choke mechanism can stick, causing rich running and poor economy
Oil seal wear - rocker cover and sump gaskets commonly leak on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for A+ MG Montego engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: The S-Series 1.6 offered balanced performance for mid-range Montegos, with improved torque over the A+ but sharing some thermal management weaknesses.
commonFailures: Thermostat housing crack - plastic housing prone to splitting, leading to overheating
Timing chain tensioner wear - causes rattle and potential oil pressure warnings at higher mileage
Inlet manifold gasket leak - rough idle and vacuum leaks common after 80k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for 20M4 MG Montego engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: The flagship S-Series 2.0 powered most Montegos, offering strong torque and the basis for the high-output MG variants, but head gasket failures are well-documented.
compatibleVariants: 2.0 L, 2.0 HLS, MG 2.0, Vanden Plas 2.0, Estate 2.0, 2.0 Carburettor
commonFailures: Head gasket failure - coolant/oil mixing common after 80k miles; MLS gasket upgrade recommended
Timing chain tensioner wear - causes rattle and potential oil pressure warnings
Thermostat housing crack - plastic housing prone to splitting, leading to overheating
entries.cta: Get quotes for 20M2F MG Montego engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol Injection
entries.history: The O-Series brought Lucas fuel injection to the Montego range, improving driveability and emissions but introducing more complex electronics for classic owners to maintain.
commonFailures: Lucas airflow meter failure - causes poor drivability, stalling and hard starting
Fuel pressure regulator wear - leads to rich running and increased fuel consumption
ECU adaptation reset required after battery disconnect - can cause rough idle if not performed
entries.cta: Get quotes for 20T4F MG Montego engine replacement

engineGuide.closing: Can't find your engine code? Every rebuilt engine comes with a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

commonProblems.h2: MG Montego Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The MG Montego's S-Series petrol and Perkins Prima diesel engines are generally robust but have well-documented failure points that drive replacement enquiries. Head gasket failures on the 2.0L S-Series and injector pump wear on the Perkins diesel are the most common issues. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.group: MG Montego S-Series Head Gasket Failure
problems.h4: MG Montego S-Series Head Gasket Failure - Why 2.0L Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: MG Montego 2.0 L, 2.0 HLS, MG 2.0, Vanden Plas 2.0, Estate 2.0 (1984-1994, S-Series 20M2F/20T2F)
problems.rootCause: Original composite head gasket degrades under thermal cycling; plastic thermostat housing cracks exacerbate overheating risk.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: MLS head gasket replacement, thermostat housing upgrade, coolant system flush
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught early before overheating damage; not suitable if head warping has occurred

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Head removal, crack testing, valve seat reconditioning, new gasket set, timing components
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if block is undamaged; extends life but doesn't address block wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with MLS gasket upgrade, tensioner fix, bench testing, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage Montegos; addresses all wear points, includes warranty

problems.recommendation: On a classic MG Montego valued at £1,500-£4,000, a full rebuilt engine often represents better long-term value than repeated repairs. If your S-Series has exceeded 90k miles or shows signs of overheating damage, replacement with an upgraded unit is the most reliable solution for restoration projects.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt S-Series engine prices for your MG Montego

problems.group: MG Montego Perkins Prima Injector Pump Failure
problems.h4: MG Montego Perkins Prima Injector Pump Failure - Diesel Starting & Power Issues
problems.affectedModels: MG Montego 2.0 Diesel, Estate 2.0 Diesel (1989-1994, Perkins 4.100); 2.0 Turbo Diesel (4.108)
problems.rootCause: High-pressure injector pump internal wear reduces fuel delivery pressure; diesel contamination accelerates wear on precision components.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Pump removal, bench calibration, seal replacement, fuel filter renewal
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for early-stage wear; won't fix severely worn internal components

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit reconditioned pump, fuel system bleed, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Good option if engine block is healthy; doesn't address other wear items

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with upgraded pump, injectors, glow plugs, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage diesels; comprehensive solution with warranty protection

problems.recommendation: Perkins diesel injector pumps are expensive to repair individually. If your Montego Diesel has hard starting, black smoke or power loss beyond 100k miles, a full rebuilt engine with upgraded fuel system components often provides better reliability and value than piecemeal repairs.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Perkins Prima rebuilt engines for your MG Montego

problems.group: MG Montego Timing Chain Tensioner Failure
problems.h4: MG Montego Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - S-Series Rattle & Oil Pressure Warning
problems.affectedModels: MG Montego 1.6 HL, 2.0 L, 2.0 HLS, MG 1.6, MG 2.0 (1984-1994, S-Series 20M4/20M2F)
problems.rootCause: Original tensioner design allows chain slack to develop; worn tensioner causes rattling noise and can lead to timing jump or oil pump drive wear.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Tensioner replacement, chain inspection, oil pressure check
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if chain and sprockets show minimal wear; preventative only

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full timing chain, tensioner, sprockets, seals replacement
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if engine is otherwise healthy; doesn't address internal wear

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt S-Series with Timing Upgrade
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with upgraded tensioner, duplex chain, oil pump inspection, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage units; addresses all timing system wear plus internal condition

problems.recommendation: If your S-Series Montego has a timing rattle and has exceeded 90k miles, the cost of a full timing kit approaches half the price of a rebuilt engine. For restoration projects, a rebuilt unit with upgraded timing components provides peace of mind and warranty coverage that a repair cannot match.
problems.cta: Compare S-Series timing-upgraded rebuilt engines for your MG Montego

problems.h4: MG Montego Thermostat Housing Crack - Overheating & Coolant Loss
problems.rootCause: Original plastic thermostat housing becomes brittle with age and heat cycling; cracks cause coolant loss leading to overheating and potential head gasket failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Plastic housing replacement, new gasket, coolant refill
repairOptions.longevity: Quick fix if caught early; upgraded aluminium housing recommended for longevity

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Housing upgrade, water pump, thermostat, hoses, radiator pressure test
repairOptions.longevity: Comprehensive preventative maintenance; suitable if engine shows no overheating damage

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Cooling Upgrade
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with aluminium housing, upgraded water pump, MLS gasket, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best if overheating has already occurred; addresses root cause and any collateral damage

problems.recommendation: The plastic thermostat housing is a known weak point. If your Montego has overheated or shows coolant loss, inspect for head gasket damage before investing in cooling repairs. For high-mileage S-Series engines, a rebuilt unit with aluminium housing upgrade is often the most reliable long-term solution.
problems.cta: Get quotes for S-Series engines with cooling upgrades for your MG Montego

problems.h4: MG Montego Carburettor Flooding - SU Carb Issues on 1.3L & 2.0L Carb Models
problems.affectedModels: MG Montego 1.3 L, 1.3 HL, 2.0 Carburettor (1984-1994, A+ and S-Series carb variants)
problems.rootCause: SU carburettor choke mechanism sticks or dashpot oil leaks; float valve wear causes fuel flooding, leading to rich running, poor starting and carbon buildup.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Bench clean, choke mechanism repair, float valve replacement, idle mixture calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for age-related sticking; won't fix worn throttle shafts or severe flooding

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full strip, new seals, needle valve, dashpot overhaul, bench test
repairOptions.longevity: Good for restoring classic carb performance; requires specialist SU knowledge

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Carb Calibration
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with calibrated SU carb, upgraded seals, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage classic Montegos; combines engine renewal with authentic carb setup

problems.recommendation: SU carburettors are serviceable but require specialist knowledge. If your classic Montego has persistent flooding or poor running after a basic service, a full carb rebuild by an SU specialist is worthwhile. For high-mileage engines, consider a rebuilt unit with authentic carb calibration for period-correct restoration.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt A+/S-Series engines with SU carb calibration for your MG Montego

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your MG Montego
finalCta.paragraph: The MG Montego's classic status makes engine preservation worthwhile, but known failures like S-Series head gaskets and Perkins injector pumps require proactive solutions. EngineMarket connects you with UK specialists who supply rebuilt engines with upgraded components addressing these specific weaknesses. Compare quotes today and keep your Montego on the road with confidence, backed by a 12-month unlimited mileage warranty.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare MG Montego Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific MG Montego and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

faq.h2: MG Montego Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: What is the most common MG Montego engine failure?
items.answer: The S-Series 2.0L petrol head gasket failure is the single most common issue, followed by Perkins diesel injector pump wear. Both typically occur between 80k-120k miles and often drive full engine replacement decisions during classic restoration projects.
keyPoints: S-Series head gasket fails due to thermal cycling and plastic thermostat housing cracks
Perkins injector pump wear causes hard starting and power loss after 100k miles
Timing chain tensioner wear on S-Series causes rattle and oil pressure warnings
MLS gasket upgrade recommended on rebuilt S-Series units for longevity
items.cta: Compare rebuilt S-Series engine prices for your MG Montego

items.question: How much does an MG Montego 2.0 engine replacement cost?
items.answer: A rebuilt MG Montego 2.0L S-Series petrol engine typically costs £850-£2,400 supply-only in the UK market. Supply & fit installation adds £450-£850 depending on location and workshop rates for classic car restoration projects.
keyPoints: Prices vary by engine condition, variant and whether upgrades are included
Rebuilt units with MLS gasket and tensioner upgrades sit at the higher end
Used engines available from £450 but carry shorter warranty periods
Always confirm warranty terms and upgrade specifications before ordering
items.cta: Get a tailored MG Montego 2.0 engine replacement quote today

items.question: Can I fit a different Rover engine into my MG Montego?
items.answer: While some Rover S-Series and O-Series engines share mounting points, compatibility depends on ECU, wiring loom and ancillary fitment. We recommend sticking to original-spec rebuilt engines for reliable classic restoration without complex conversion work.
keyPoints: S-Series 1.6L and 2.0L share basic architecture but differ in ancillaries
O-Series injection engines require matching ECU and sensor compatibility checks
Perkins diesel swaps need fuel system and exhaust modifications
Original-spec rebuilt engines avoid conversion complications for classics
items.cta: Check MG Montego engine compatibility using your registration number

items.question: What warranty comes with a rebuilt MG Montego engine?
items.answer: All rebuilt MG Montego engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists. Terms vary by supplier, so always confirm coverage details before ordering for your classic restoration project.
keyPoints: Warranty covers manufacturing defects and premature failure of rebuilt components
Unlimited mileage terms benefit high-use classic car enthusiasts
Some suppliers offer extended warranty options for additional cost
Keep service records and use recommended oils to maintain warranty validity
items.cta: Compare MG Montego rebuilt engine warranty terms from UK specialists

items.question: How long does MG Montego engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only rebuilt engines typically dispatch within 3-7 working days after order confirmation. Full supply & fit installation usually requires 2-4 days workshop time depending on variant complexity and garage scheduling for classic Montego restoration projects.
keyPoints: Engine build time varies by specialist workload and upgrade requirements
Allow extra time for shipping to remote UK locations
Supply & fit bookings should be scheduled in advance with your chosen garage
Post-installation run-in and diagnostics add half a day to the process
items.cta: Book your MG Montego engine supply & fit appointment with UK specialists

items.question: Are used MG Montego engines reliable?
items.answer: Used MG Montego engines can be reliable if sourced from low-mileage, well-maintained donors and thoroughly tested before supply. However, rebuilt units with upgraded components addressing known failures offer greater peace of mind for classic car restoration investments.
keyPoints: Always request compression and leak-down test results before purchasing
Verify donor vehicle history and mileage documentation where possible
Used engines typically carry shorter warranty periods than rebuilt units
For high-value MG variants, rebuilt engines often represent better long-term value
items.cta: Compare used vs rebuilt MG Montego engine options and pricing

items.question: What's the difference between reconditioned and rebuilt MG Montego engines?
items.answer: Reconditioned MG Montego engines receive cleaning, inspection and targeted repairs to worn parts, while rebuilt engines undergo comprehensive renewal with upgraded components addressing known failure points like head gaskets and timing systems.
keyPoints: Reconditioned: cost-effective for engines with low to moderate wear
Rebuilt: includes MLS gaskets, upgraded tensioners, renewed internals
Rebuilt units typically carry longer warranty coverage
Choose rebuilt for high-mileage or performance MG Montego variants
items.cta: Compare reconditioned vs rebuilt MG Montego engine prices and specs

items.question: Can I get an MG Montego MG 2.0 engine if mine has failed?
items.answer: Yes, MG 2.0 S-Series engines are available as rebuilt units through EngineMarket's specialist network, though demand from enthusiasts can affect availability and pricing. Early enquiry is recommended for rare performance variant restoration projects.
keyPoints: MG 2.0 engines command premium pricing due to enthusiast restoration demand
Rebuilt units include performance-spec components where original was uprated
Allow extra lead time for sourcing rare performance variant engines
Confirm engine code and variant specifications match your original unit
items.cta: Enquire about MG Montego MG 2.0 rebuilt engine availability today

items.question: Do you supply MG Montego 2.0 Diesel Perkins Prima engines?
items.answer: Yes, both naturally aspirated 4.100 and turbocharged 4.108 Perkins Prima diesel engines are available as rebuilt units for MG Montego Diesel variants. Upgraded injector pumps and glow plug systems are included where original weaknesses are documented.
keyPoints: Rebuilt Perkins engines include injector pump calibration and seal renewal
Turbo variants receive wastegate inspection and boost system checks
Diesel rebuilt prices reflect specialist rebuilding complexity and parts scarcity
Confirm fuel type and turbo specification when requesting quotes
items.cta: Get quotes for MG Montego Perkins Prima rebuilt diesel engines

items.question: How do I find my MG Montego engine code?
items.answer: Your MG Montego engine code is stamped on the engine block near the cylinder head, listed on your V5C logbook under "engine number", or can be instantly verified by entering your registration number in our DVLA-linked compatibility tool above.
keyPoints: V5C logbook lists engine number but not always the factory code
Our compatibility tool cross-references registration to exact engine specification
Providing the correct code ensures you receive compatible replacement quotes
